examples: I'm definitely coming to the party.
This is definitely the best pizza I've ever had.
You should definitely try the cheesecake.
context: During the pandemic a lot of students found themselves scrolling a lot through their phones. They say it shortens attention span and it kind of takes away from creativity. Um you're watching other people do things when you yourself could be creating and a lot of that falls on parents too. I I believe that's my opinion. If you're allowing your kid to spend seven to eight hours a day on on a cell phone, probably not the best thing to do. I think with anything, you have to have a balance. Okay. A little time for social media, a little time for a game here and there, but definitely responsibilities have to come into play.

A lot of skills that you use later in life, you learn as an adolescent. You learn responsibility. You learn time management. You learn all that as you're growing up. So that helps you later in life to hold down a job to have good morals. Another point they made is that a lot of students see school as maybe a secondary option rather than a learning opportunity and they're taking school too lightly. I can't really say anything about that. When I was in school, I took school way too lightly. Looking back, I wish I would have taken it more serious, but just the mischievous boy in me wanted to skip school and not worry about school, thinking it's not going to affect me later in life, where now I know it definitely affects you later in life. Thank God for them teachers that I had that went over and beyond. Teachers, in my opinion, nowadays, they're not regarded like they used to be. there's a teacher shortage. So, you have unqualified teachers teaching or you might have one teacher teaching three different subjects rather than specializing. It's a problem. I I guess I can't stress enough, balance, balance, balance. And a lot of it takes discipline. I couldn't pay attention for more than minutes when I was growing up.
